figures.caption: Table 1. Main results on LIBERO benchmark, reported as mean ± std over 5 seeds for the methods we run. †: reproduced with our settings. Best results in bold, second best underlined.

Figure 5. Emergent capabilities from online adaptation across diverse LIBERO tasks. (a) Error Recovery: On the milk-to-basket task, the policy detects a slip and autonomously re-adjusts its gripper. (b) Adaptive Object Handling: On the stove activation task, the policy adapts its trajectory after the knob is displaced. (c) Novel Strategy Discovery: On the bowl-from-stove task, LGE guidance leads to a side-approach grasp not present in demonstrations. (d) Exploration Suggestions: On the drawer-andbowl task, the critic suggests opening the drawer before grasping, avoiding a common failure mode.
Figure 3. Learning curves on all LIBERO suites. Shaded regions denote ±1 std over 5 seeds.
Table 5. Ablation study on LIBERO-Long task suite.

Table 6. Controlled comparisons on LIBERO-Long under a matched rollout and optimization budget. Each curriculum/exploration choice replaces (rather than removes) the corresponding component. Capability-aware ARS and languageguided LGE remain the strongest configuration.

Figure 1. Framework overview. The adaptation loop begins with Experience Memory retrieving relevant parameters for warm initialization. During interaction, the VLA receives structured guidance from Language-Guided Exploration, which generates promptbased hints to aid diverse behavior discovery. Trajectories are evaluated by Adaptive Reward Synthesis, which dynamically weighs sub-goals based on the agent’s real-time capability estimates. This capability-aware mechanism naturally forms an auto-curriculum, providing stable dense feedback for GRPO optimization.

Table 8. Results on RoboTwin 2.0 (dual-arm, Aloha AgileX). Policies are trained with 50 clean demonstrations per task and evaluated with 100 rollouts; the Hard setting adds domain randomization (clutter, lighting, texture, height). We report a representative task subset and the subset average; Agentic-VLA is strongest in both Easy and Hard settings.
